title: Senior Designer

We partner with over 300 clients in 40+ countries and counting. Our unique model drives creativity and efficiency, allowing us to deliver tailored solutions that resonate deeply with audiences. As a part of The Brandtech Group , we're at the forefront of leveraging cutting-edge AI technology to revolutionise how we create and deliver work. Our AI solutions enhance efficiency, spark creativity, and drive insightful decision-making, empowering our teams to produce innovative and impactful results. We're looking for someone who combines strong conceptual thinking with hands-on design craft — someone who can develop a big idea and execute it beautifully across social, digital, and, where needed, OOH and POS touchpoints. You'll guide and mentor a team of designers and content creators, elevating the standard of social-first design while integrating Gen AI tools thoughtfully into the creative process. title: Creative Director - Collabs and Partnerships
description: Requirements Description & Requirements who we are lululemon is an innovative performance apparel company for yoga, running, training, and other athletic pursuits. Setting the bar in technical fabrics and functional design, we create transformational products and experiences that support people in moving, growing, connecting, and being well. We owe our success to our innovative product, emphasis on stores, commitment to our people, and the incredible connections we make in every community we're in. As a company, we focus on creating positive change to build a healthier, thriving future. In particular, that includes creating an equitable, inclusive and growth-focused environment for our people. About This Team We are a crew of creatives centered in curiosity and craft, playfulness and purpose, bringing a new vision of wellbeing to life. We inspire and educate by meaningfully expressing the mind/body/community connection. In our role to drive brand distinction around the world, we focus on distinction through rich storytelling, embracing long-term concepts, and obsessing our craft. Core Responsibilities The Global Brand Creative Director – Collabs and Partnerships leads the creative vision, strategy, art direction, and execution for lululemon’s global collaborations. This leader elevates brand storytelling across campaigns, activities, branded initiatives, identity, and omni‑channel experiences. They guide internal teams and external agencies to ensure every creative expression is distinctive, consistent, and aligned with lululemon’s purpose, aesthetic, and long‑term brand vision. This role requires a visionary creative leader who thrives at the intersection of culture, sport, fashion, and community — and who can translate that energy into breakthrough creative work that drives brand love, cultural relevance, and business impact. Global Brand Creative Leadership Elevate lululemon’s global brand creative output across campaigns, activities, branded initiatives, identity, and omni‑channel experiences, with a dedicated focus on the Partnerships and Collaborations portfolio. Champion creative excellence and ensure all work reflects lululemon’s values, aesthetic, and long‑term brand vision. Lead the overall creative direction and art direction for collaboration‑driven storytelling. Creative Strategy Development Define and evolve lululemon’s creative strategy across activities and platforms to strengthen brand distinction, cohesion, and cultural resonance. Identify opportunities for creative consistency across channels, ensuring unified brand expression globally and regionally. Build creative frameworks that support scalable storytelling across diverse collaboration partners. Brand & Product Storytelling Partner with Brand Strategy to define and shape brand and product storytelling opportunities that deepen connection and drive relevance. Use market trends, consumer insights, and competitive landscape analysis to inform and elevate creative direction. Translate cultural, sport, and fashion signals into compelling narratives that differentiate lululemon’s collaborations. Art Direction Leadership Lead the art direction for all collaboration‑related creative, ensuring visual storytelling is elevated, cohesive, and reflective of lululemon’s aesthetic standards. Direct the look, feel, and visual identity of campaigns, shoots, and omni‑channel experiences — from concept through final execution. Guide teams in developing elevated visual systems that bring collaboration partners and lululemon’s brand values together in a distinctive, ownable way. Ensure all visual expressions meet the highest standards of craft, clarity, and cultural relevance. Agency Leadership Identify, manage, and direct global creative agencies and partners, ensuring world‑class creative development and execution. Provide clear direction, feedback, and alignment to external partners to deliver breakthrough creative work. Maintain strong relationships with agencies and par
score_reason: Senior creative leadership at established brand with global scope, but no remote/location confirmation, no salary posted, and brand/lifestyle focus vs. Travis's integrated campaign expertise creates slight misalignment.

title: Creative Director
description: GLOBAL HOSPITALITY SOCIAL AOR Creative Director Coverage: Flagship loyalty brand Commitment: ~40 hours/week Rate: $80/hour Start: Immediate 1099 contract through Dec 2026, with potential to convert to W-2 in 2027 You set the creative bar for the social presence of one of the world's largest hospitality brands, day in and day out. Our Senior Creative Director is on the account part-time, which means that day to day, you are the most senior creative presence on the account. The feed is yours to make excellent. ABOUT JACOB MEDIA Jacob Media is a social-first storytelling studio, a team of creatives, strategists, and producers harnessing their collective talent to set a new standard for storytelling on social, informed by insights and cultural trends. Built to plug in as a true extension of a brand's in-house team. We work with the likes of Lamborghini, Etihad, Delta, Sonos, and Bugatti, based between Los Angeles and Dubai. We are intentionally lean and independent, which lets us move quickly to keep up with what's happening in culture and set the trends ourselves. THE ROLE You own the creative output of the account from concept through to ship, at a pace of up to 50 assets a month across six platforms. That means leading the creative thinking against the monthly content plan, briefing and reviewing the work of the social team, designer, copywriter, and video editor, holding the visual and tonal standard, and making sure the feed feels like one cohesive brand rather than a stack of one-off posts. On day-to-day creative decisions, the call is yours. You partner with our Head of Creative Strategy on what we say, with the Account Director on how it lands with the client, and you escalate to our Senior Creative Director on the big swings. We are hiring you to make decisions, not to route them. YOUR FIRST 90 DAYS Absorb brand systems and get fluent in what the client considers on-brand at this level. Take over creative reviews and set the standard for what ships and what goes back. Ship your first full months of always-on content across the calendar. Build the working rhythm with the designer, copywriter, and editor so the team moves fast without dropping craft. WHAT YOU'LL OWN The creative output of the account, from concept through to the look, feel, voice, and pace of what ships. Leading the social, design, copy, and video team's creative thinking, and raising the quality of every idea before it goes out. Translating cultural and brand moments into on-brand ideas fast, without losing brand discipline. Creative reviews, feedback, and the standard the team holds itself to. Partnering with the Head of Creative Strategy and Account Director so the work is both beautiful and on brief. Working with the Analytics Lead so creative decisions are informed by what is actually working in the feed. WHAT YOU BRING 7+ years in social and creative, with senior creative ownership on a premium global brand. A portfolio that proves you can make luxury or hospitality social that stops the scroll and still respects the brand. A genuinely social-first instinct. You think in platforms, formats, and culture, not repurposed campaign assets. The ability to lead and elevate a team, not just produce great work yourself. Comfort holding the creative standard while moving at the pace of an always-on account shipping up to 50 assets a month. BONUS POINTS Hands-on craft (art direction, motion sensibility, editing eye) that lets you prototype an idea, not just describe it. Experience directing creators or working within a creator network. Multi-market sensitivity: creative that lands in Asia, Europe, the Middle East, and the Americas. 1 opening. US-based, remote welcome. Holds US working hours. THE FINE PRINT This is a 1099 independent contractor engagement, not employment. You invoice us, you handle your own taxes and insurance, and you bring your own kit. The contract runs through December 31, 2026 and is guaranteed for that term. If the work is exceptional and t
score_reason: Senior creative leadership at a hospitality brand with interesting scope, but part-time contract work ($80/hr, 40hrs/week) and social-first focus sit outside traditional advertising—interesting adjacent play but unclear if it meets Travis's senior leadership trajectory.

title: Creative Director
description: Job Description About Acrisure A global fintech leader, Acrisure empowers millions of ambitious businesses and individuals with the right solutions to grow boldly forward. Bringing cutting-edge technology and top-tier human support together, we connect clients with customized solutions across a range of insurance, reinsurance, payroll, benefits, cybersecurity, mortgage services – and more. In the last twelve years, Acrisure has grown in revenue from $38 million to almost $5 billion and employs over 19,000 colleagues in more than 20 countries. Acrisure was built on entrepreneurial spirit. Prioritizing leadership, accountability, and collaboration, we equip our teams to work at the highest levels possible. Job Summary Acrisure is seeking a Creative Director to build and lead the creative function that will define how Acrisure looks, speaks, and shows up in the world. This is a foundational hire, reporting directly to the CMO, for a company that has scaled to nearly $5 billion in revenue and is now ready to build the brand that reflects its ambition. The number one skill of a great Creative Director is having sharper instincts for what makes work great, and the authority and decisiveness to act on them. The mandate begins with brand: visual identity, design systems, voice, and a web experience worthy of a global fintech leader, extending over time into integrated campaigns, demand generation, and full-funnel creative output across every touchpoint. You'll own the creative vision end-to-end, leading a multi-layered, global creative organization, directing agency and vendor relationships, and ensuring creative drives measurable business outcomes, including growth, differentiation, and engagement by producing the work yourself, not just approving it. This role extends beyond Marketing to influence how Acrisure shows up across Product, Sales, Corporate Communications, and Executive Leadership. This is a rare opportunity to define what a Fortune-caliber insurance and fintech brand looks and sounds like, with direct C-suite access and the scale to make your work matter globally. Responsibilities: Own Brand Identity & Visual Systems Define Acrisure's brand identity - visual language, design system, typography, color, motion, voice, and tone - building a coherent creative foundation that scales across products, markets, and audiences. Own Acrisure's web experience as a living brand touchpoint - leading the initial redesign and continuing to evolve it, so it reflects the scale, sophistication, and ambition of the business over time. Establish brand standards, guidelines, and governance frameworks that enable internal teams, business-unit marketing teams, and external partners to produce on-brand work consistently and at speed across the organization. Bring craft and intentionality to every brand touchpoint, from executive presentations and event experiences to social content and sales collateral. Lead Creative Strategy & Storytelling Develop compelling brand narratives that make Acrisure's complex, multi-product portfolio accessible, differentiated, and memorable across audiences - from enterprise buyers to individual clients. Partner with the CMO to shape Acrisure’s brand strategy and translate it into creative strategy, then translate creative strategy into exceptional work. Lead creative for flagship events, sponsorships, and high-visibility brand moments. As the function matures, extend creative ownership into integrated demand generation campaigns, performance creative, and full-funnel marketing programs. Build & Lead the Creative Organization Serve as Acrisure's creative conscience - championing great work, raising the bar, and building a culture where craft and strategic thinking go hand in hand. Build and lead a multi-layer creative organization including senior leaders across disciplines (e.g. design, content, production). Oversee the ecosystem of agency relationships aligned to enterprise strategy, ensuring external partners del

title: Senior Director, Creative

We seek to ignite that passion by setting high standards, championing teamwork, and creating work-life harmony as we collectively navigate rapid growth on a global scale while striving for greater equity and opportunity – inside and outside our organization. To flourish here, you must be prepared to set a high bar for yourself and those around you. There is always a way to contribute: Acting with autonomy, having accountability and being open to new perspectives are essential to our continued success. Our deep curiosity to learn and our eagerness to share diverse passions with others gives us balance and injects a one-of-a-kind vibrancy into our culture. If you are driven to solve exhilarating challenges and have a bias toward action in the face of change, you will be empowered to make a real impact here, with a sharp and passionate team at your back. If Braze sounds like a place where you can thrive, we can’t wait to meet you. This role is responsible not only for visual excellence, but for building, developing, and maintaining a high-performing design team grounded in strong conceptual thinking. Success in this role depends on the ability to set a clear creative bar, move decisively when standards aren’t met, and actively mentor emerging leaders so responsibility and ownership can be pushed downward—freeing this role to operate up, out, and across the organization. The Senior Director, Creative partners closely with strategy, brand, and creative leadership to ensure our visual systems are insight-led, scalable, and emotionally resonant. title: Head of Brand
description: About Primer Primer is a growing network of K–8 schools restoring the timeless foundations of American education. Inspired by the original teaching primers that shaped a nation of readers, thinkers, and builders, our schools begin with what has always worked, and build for what lies ahead. Close-knit learning environments. Skilled educators. A culture that takes kids seriously as learners and leaders. Primers shaped the first 200 years of American children. Primer will ready them for the next. About The Role Primer has all the makings of an iconic brand: a big vision to solve one of the hardest problems in America, real stories of lives we’ve changed across the country, and strong cultural tailwinds pushing us forward. We hear from parents, educators, legislators, and community leaders every day about the lives we’re changing — from families who felt stuck in the traditional system and now see their child thriving at Primer, to parents who “feel like they have their child back” after a few months at Primer. Your job is to harness this momentum and craft a brand that millions of families from all walks of life trust with the most important decision they make each year: their child’s school. You will own the entire surface area of Primer’s brand — from our big-picture narrative to individual brand assets across visual design, photography, film content, and copy. You’ll inherit our existing brand systems, which you see today across Primer.com, and be tasked with growing this foundation into the future of Primer’s brand. There Are Three Unique Challenges In This Role First, our brand must be both aspirational and accessible. Most education brands opt for pure exclusivity or pure accessibility, but that won’t work for Primer. Second, we are a central marketing & brand org with dozens (and soon hundreds) of local Primers. These campuses are the primary way families experience the Primer brand post-enrollment, and nailing the local manifestation of our brand will require systems that scale down to (and empower) those working at the campus level. Finally, you’ll need to develop (or have) a nuanced intuition for how Primer should fit into the overall education ecosystem. Education is tough, riddled with many strong opinions, which presents both a challenge and an opportunity. If you shape our public perception with conviction — and succeed — you will have crafted one of the all-time great American brands. Responsibilities Define what Primer stands for. Own our narrative — where we fit in the broader education ecosystem, how we show up, what we're for and what we're against. Craft our brand norms. These are the standards that define our voice, language, and visual identity. The kind of thing a campus team or a writer can actually pick up and use, not a deck that sits in a drive. Build the local engine. We have hundreds (and soon thousands) of local educators who are the face of Primer to our families. Creating a consistent brand experience is a unique challenge, and you will own it end-to-end. Consistency without bottlenecks. Build the operating system that dials in every public-facing artifact we ship. Fight the approval-chain-by-vibes with clear standards and lightweight checks against them. Partner with Growth, Admissions, and Product to establish the messaging, identity, and creative standards used across marketing, admissions, and the product experience, enabling each team to execute within those standards. Lay the groundwork for employer brand and PR, so our story lands with parents, future staff, and the public alike. What we're looking for A strong operator who loves building systems. You're clear-eyed about the complexity of a central brand function with dozens of local spokes. Someone who's done a lot with a little. You've held a high bar in a resource-constrained environment. A brand leader who’s done it before — you’ve built brands that feel both aspirational and accessible, and understand what it takes to connect with every
score_reason: Senior brand leadership with strong creative scope, but no salary listed, no remote confirmation, and education sector (not advertising/marketing core) creates notable gaps.

title: Senior Vice President, Brand Strategy
description: Company Description Heartbeat is a full-service agency for healthcare Challenger Brands. Challengers are playing a high-stakes game—they must battle behemoth market leaders, upend ingrained treatment habits, or transform a “me-too” product into “yes-please.” The agency is known for serving up distinctive strategies and differentiating approaches, ensuring clients’ market positions are uniquely owned. Heartbeat designs their organization around a 100% integration model, housing media, strategy, med comms, creative, technology, and even point of care/population health under the same roof for the tightest, most integrated campaigns possible. They price work based on outcomes, not outputs. Their innovative model delivers not only alignment, but powerful orchestration, greater efficiency, and maximum impact for every dollar spent—all the necessary elements for Challengers to triumph. That’s the philosophy, here’s the creds: 250+ employees between NYC & LA; 6 agency leaders with decades of tenure; a pile of awards from the likes of the Mannys, MM&M Awards, OMMAs, and Clios; an industry-leading commitment to diversity and inclusion (with a Med Ad News D&I Champion nod to match); and a President who dared to be quoted as saying, “We celebrate people who stand up and say, ‘You know what? This is dumb.' Damn straight. Overview The SVP, Brand Strategy is a senior strategic leader responsible for shaping brand strategy across oncology‑focused pharmaceutical work. This role partners closely with senior client stakeholders and internal cross‑functional teams to define strategic direction, guide complex decision‑making, and ensure work ladders back to clear, meaningful business outcomes. This leader will set the strategic bar for the work, serve as a trusted thought partner to senior clients, and mentor a growing team of strategists. Success in this role requires deep pharmaceutical brand experience, comfort navigating ambiguity, and the ability to balance long‑term vision with day‑to‑day operational rigor. Responsibilities Strategic Leadership & Brand Stewardship Lead brand strategy across one or more oncology brands, indications, or portfolio segments, serving as the strategic authority for the work. Own the core strategic narrative, including positioning, segmentation, target definition, brand architecture, and long‑term roadmap development. Translate complex clinical, competitive, and market dynamics into clear, actionable strategic direction. Ensure all downstream work—creative, messaging, channels, and experiences—remains grounded in a coherent and defensible strategy. Client Partnership Act as a senior strategic advisor to brand, marketing, and cross‑functional client team members. Proactively anticipate client needs, identify white space, and surface opportunities to strengthen brand performance. Lead high‑stakes conversations, workshops, and presentations with confidence and credibility. Partner with client teams on qualitative and quantitative research efforts and apply insights appropriately to brand decisions. Cross‑Functional Collaboration Work in close partnership with Creative, Account, Experience, Medical, and Analytics teams to deliver integrated brand solutions. Guide and inspire creative teams through clear briefs, focused problem definition, and ongoing strategic stewardship. Ensure strategy is pragmatic, executable, and grounded in real‑world brand and regulatory constraints. Team Leadership & Development Lead and develop a team of strategists, providing clear direction, candid feedback, and growth opportunities. Set standards for critical thinking, brief quality, and client engagement across the strategy rigor. Coach junior and mid‑level strategists on both craft and presence, helping them progress as operators and leaders. Contribute to the evolution of Heartbeat’s strategic guidelines and points of view. Agency & Growth Contributions Participate in new business efforts, portfolio expansion, and organic growth initiati

title: VP, Brand Management
description: Who We Are Parenting isn't picture-perfect. It's messy, hilarious, exhausting, and life-changing — sometimes all before noon. That's where we come in. Founded in 2014 by our CEO Chelsea Hirschhorn, Frida was built to make the raw reality of parenting a little easier to navigate (and a lot less overwhelming). It all started with one legendary snot-sucker — the NoseFrida — and has grown into a 200+ product lineup that supports families through every stage: from fertility and postpartum recovery to baby care and beyond. We don't shy away from the stuff no one else wants to talk about — nipple pain, diaper blowouts, or the emotional rollercoaster that comes with keeping a tiny human alive. We call it like it is, solve the problems that actually matter, and build products that help parents feel seen, supported, and totally capable. You can now find Frida products in 50+ countries and thousands of stores across the U.S., from the biggest national retailers to your neighborhood grocery aisle. Under Hirschhorn's leadership, Frida has become a category leader by challenging taboos, championing honesty, and supporting families at every stage of parenthood and beyond, earning acclaim on TIME's 100 Most Influential Companies, TIME Best Inventions, Fast Company Most Innovative Companies and Fast Company Brands That Matter. But the real win? Knowing we're helping parents everywhere feel a little more human and a little less alone. How You Will Make an Impact Reporting to the SVP, Brand Management, the VP, Brand Management will lead our commercial initiatives for Frida brands and be responsible for managing and growing brands by defining strategy and driving growth initiatives across the existing and new lines of products. The VP, Brand Management will partner cross functionally to develop a robust innovation pipeline of new products and enhancements to drive brand expansion, revenue, and margin. The ideal candidate will be passionate about consumer and market dynamics and will utilize those insights to develop innovative solutions that allow us to win in the marketplace. Responsibilities to include: Meeting sales and profitability targets Spearhead the annual business review and brand planning processes Serve as a catalyst to the brand's strategic initiatives, communication plans, and product innovations across all consumer touch points Uphold the brand's positioning by demonstrating a deep understanding of key brand traits along with an understanding of a constantly evolving retail/customer environment Apply consumer insights, research, and competitive intelligence to identify new opportunities and needs in the market to support the expansion, customization, and optimization of existing products and introduction of new products Develop compelling, marketable category concepts and stories that align with overall brand vision Lead execution of product marketing strategy including product architecture, pricing strategy, and SKU management Support Line Reviews demonstrating shopper analytics, category & department insights, POS performance metrics, planogram assessments, and internal trend information Partner with Sales team to manage retailer strategies and buyer relationships Promote a high performance culture by setting clear expectations and individual goals, and focus the team/company on key drivers that impact success Partner with internal Marketing Activation/Creative team to develop communication ideas and support creative collateral to support the brand's goals Identify consumers' needs and wants for Frida products, and provide inputs to R&D to enhance current product offering and design and develop new products Other duties as assigned What You Will Need Bachelor's Degree in Business, Marketing, or related field 10-12+ years of proven experience in brand management within the consumer-packaged goods industry 3-5+ years of experience in a leadership/management role Experience in conducting, analyzing, and developing strategies based o

title: Head of Sponsorships

title: VP of Production
description: About Us Betches Media is the ultimate digital media and lifestyle destination for women. As a pioneer in the humor and entertainment space, we’ve built a powerful platform that connects with over 250M fans across social media, podcasts, web, events, and e-commerce. Our funny, honest, and candid approach resonates with women globally, creating a one-of-a-kind community. Now as a part of LBG Media, Betches is in an exciting new chapter, leveraging the global reach and innovation of our parent company to push the boundaries of creative media. This is your opportunity to be at the forefront of shaping the future of Betches Media and propel the brand through its next phase of growth. Description Betches is looking for a VP of Production to sit at the center of our next phase of creative growth — a visionary builder who is excited by the challenge of turning big, culture-driving ideas into world-class execution at scale. This role is equal parts creative partner, strategic operator, and production architect: someone who helps our teams dream bigger, then designs the systems, workflows, and processes that make those ideas real. As the senior-most production leader across the company, the VP of Production will oversee production and post-production while partnering closely with creative and business leads to bring projects of every size to life — from social series to podcasts to premium, multi-platform franchises. You’ll play a defining role in how Betches operates as an entertainment brand, translating creative vision into clear production strategies, budgets, and timelines, and ensuring our output is intentional, performance-minded, and unmistakably Betches. title: AI Creative Lead

This role suits someone genuinely curious — energized by tools that didn’t exist last year, restless with “how we’ve always done it,” and as comfortable chasing a craft detail as chasing a new technique. You’re opinionated about the work but open-minded about the methods, and you’d rather figure out what’s newly possible than wait to be told. Lead concept development across projects, setting creative direction and bringing a strong, differentiated point of view. Pioneer new creative approaches that AI makes possible — unlocking ideas, formats, and executions that weren’t feasible before. Work fluidly at every scale of the work — from complete assets to individual creative fragments and components — creating, modifying, iterating, and finishing as the project demands, to a polished professional standard. Stay ahead of a fast-moving landscape — exploring new AI tools as they emerge and setting the standard for how they’re used to make exceptional creative work. Partner with business stakeholders to translate their goals — and the need for speed — into creative output, using AI to get there. Steward quality, brand consistency, and the rights/ethical considerations that come with AI-generated content. Maintain creative momentum, anticipating and resolving production issues before they escalate. Essential Requirements Strong creative sensibility with a proven ability to evaluate, direct, and elevate creative work. Ability to produce high-quality content quickly using AI, with the skill to get the best results from available models, even when preferred tools or access are restricted. Deep, current fluency with generative AI tools across your medium — plus the craft to take their output the last mile: catching where AI falls short, and refining imagery, video, and voiceover so the final work feels intentional and unmistakably human. Hands-on experience with leading AI models and creative tools — including Adobe, Google, Jasper, Claude, and Synthesia — and the adaptability to master new ones as the landscape shifts. A track record of pushing creative methods forward — developing new approaches, not just applying existing ones. An experimental mindset that runs ahead of the curve — you don’t just adopt new tools quickly, you probe what they make newly possible, and you aren’t attached to any single one. Exceptional communication skills — verbal, written, and presentation — including the ability to bring others along and influence without authority. A portfolio demonstrating both creative judgment and command of AI tools, ideally with visible process rather than only polished end results. Preferred Qualifications Experience owning creative concept development on significant projects or campaigns — agency, studio, or in-house. A track record of inventing new creative formats or approaches with AI — work that others go on to build from. Fluency across more than one creative medium (e.g., still, motion, design, copy), or the range to direct across them. Some familiarity with the rights, licensing, and provenance landscape around AI-generated content.

You will report to the VP of Marketing and work as part of a lean group that sets the strategy and ships the work. Role Overview Halfday’s binding constraint isn’t persuasion — it’s exposure. This is a builder’s role for a brand-led marketer who can sharpen how Halfday shows up everywhere consumers and retailers meet it, and keep our product launches moving on time. You will own the modern tea brand platform — positioning, voice, campaign system, creative standards, PR, organic social, and creator strategy — and run the integrated marketing calendar that keeps Brand, Growth, and Retail in rhythm. Through product development you will also serve as marketing’s launch PM, coordinating briefs, packaging inputs, claims, and creative routing until each item hands off to Retail & Shopper for commercialization. Organic Social & Creative Pipeline: Own day-to-day organic social and the brand and campaign creative pipeline — briefing and shipping work with internal and external partners, and holding the standard even where production runs through partners. Creator / UGC Strategy: Build the creator engine and content standards, partnering with Growth so creator and PR assets are whitelisting-ready for paid social, TikTok Shop, and retail media. PR & Brand Narrative: Manage earned media, founder and brand storytelling, and the narrative that supports retailer and Series B moments. Brand Health: Track awareness, message recall, sentiment, and perception — the leading indicators that show the brand is gaining traction. Qualifications Experience: 6–8+ years in brand, content, creative strategy, or integrated marketing — ideally CPG/beverage or a high-growth consumer brand. Proven Track Record: Built or meaningfully evolved a brand platform, campaign system, or creative engine from a low base; scaling a brand through a Series B or comparable inflection is a strong plus. Craft: Strong writing, taste, and strategic judgment; can turn positioning into briefs, packaging direction, content, and retailer-facing storytelling. Operating Discipline: Launch and project-management rigor — calendars, owners, status, approvals, and cross-functional follow-up — plus experience managing creative, PR, creator, production, or social partners without overcomplicating the process. title: Head of Growth & Marketing
description: BFF Robots is not a robotics company in the traditional sense. We are a platform. Anyone a consumer, a creator, a business, a healthcare operator can go to bffrobots.com, upload a photo or type a description, and we manufacture a custom humanoid robot built around their vision. Starting at $2,509. On top of the hardware, we are launching Genesis Humanoid Control an AI operating system that gives every BFF Robot a persistent identity, memory, and voice. Your robot remembers you. It grows with you. It becomes yours. We are at the beginning of something massive. And we need a Head of Growth & Marketing who can build the engine that takes us from early momentum to household name. What You Will Own Consumer Growth (B2C): Turn the BFF Robots creator experience into a viral loop. Every person who designs a custom robot on our platform is a story worth telling. You will build the content strategy, creator partnerships, and social presence that makes BFF Robots the most talked-about brand in robotics across TikTok, Instagram, YouTube, and beyond. Creator & Influencer Ecosystem: Identify, recruit, and build long-term relationships with creators, makers, futurists, and tech enthusiasts who will become BFF Robots ambassadors. This is not influencer marketing as usual these are co-creators who will design robots, document the experience, and bring their audiences into the BFF Robots universe. B2B & Enterprise Pipeline: Partner directly with the founder to build awareness and inbound demand among healthcare operators, retail chains, hospitality groups, and government agencies. You will develop the case studies, thought leadership content, and direct outreach campaigns that open doors at the C-suite level. Brand & Community: Own the BFF Robots brand voice across every channel. Build the community of robot builders, Genesis users, and EIR partners that makes BFF Robots more than a product — a movement. Revenue: This role is tied directly to sales. You will own the top of the funnel and be accountable for qualified leads, conversion rates, and revenue attribution across all three segments. What We Are Looking For 3–7 years of experience in growth marketing, brand marketing, or content strategy — ideally at a consumer tech, hardware, or high-growth startup A proven track record of building audiences from scratch and converting them into customers Deep fluency in short-form video, creator culture, and social platform dynamics. You know what makes something spread and go viral Experience with B2B marketing and enterprise content is a strong plus Data-driven: you track what works, cut what does not, and optimize relentlessly A genuine passion for robotics, AI, or emerging technology. You need to believe in what you are selling Self-directed and entrepreneurial: this is a high-autonomy role at an early-stage company; you will set the strategy and execute it What You Get A front-row seat at a company building something genuinely new the first consumer humanoid robot platform with AI identity and persistent memory Direct access to the founder and full creative latitude to build the marketing function from the ground up Competitive base compensation + performance bonuses tied to revenue milestones Equity participation — you are building this with us, not just for us Remote-first with flexibility The chance to tell a story that has never been told before This is not a job for someone who wants to manage a content calendar. This is a job for someone who wants to build a brand that changes how the world thinks about robots.

title: Vice President, Customer Digital Experience (CDX)

As a leading global aesthetics business, our award-winning portfolio of injectables, devices, and skincare products empowers healthcare professionals to enhance confidence through aesthetic medicine. Live your best life with Merz Aesthetics. A Brief Overview The Vice President, Customer Digital Experience (CDX) is the executive accountable for defining, governing, and delivering a globally consistent, journey-led digital experience for Merz Aesthetics' customers. This role brings consumer-grade journey discipline and marketing rigor into a regulated life sciences environment, shaping how customers experience, engage with, and derive value from Merz Aesthetics across digital, field, and service interactions. The VP is accountable for ensuring customer digital experiences are intuitive, connected, compliant, and deliver measurable business impact, including engagement, growth, efficiency, and loyalty. Ensure journeys are consistent across digital, field, marketing, and service interactions. Digital Channels, Platforms & Ecosystem: Provide strategic leadership across Merz’s customer facing digital ecosystem (e.g., portals, learning platforms, marketing experiences, service tools). Partner closely with IT, Marketing, Commercial, and Data teams to shape platform strategy, integration, and modernization. Influence vendor selection and roadmap decisions to support journey based outcomes. Ensure scalability, reliability, and usability across regions and brands. Marketing Led Engagement & Adoption: Apply consumer and FMCG marketing discipline to digital experience design, driving: Clear value propositions Strong adoption of digital channels Meaningful, ongoing customer engagement Partner with Brand and Commercial teams to embed digital experience into go to market strategies. Ensure digital experiences meaningfully reshape and elevate field-based and relationship-driven commercial models, rather than merely complementing them. Measurement, Insights & Performance: Define and own the global CDX measurement framework, linking customer experience to growth, retention, efficiency and brand outcomes. This includes: Engagement and adoption metrics Journey effectiveness and drop off Satisfaction and effort Contribution to growth, retention, and efficiency Use analytics to prioritize investments and guide decision making. Regularly report progress and outcomes to executive leadership. title: B2B Marketing Director
description:
Headquarters:  Remote
      URL:   http://runwayml.com 
 

 We are building AI to simulate the world through merging art and science.  We believe that world models are at the frontier of progress in artificial intelligence. Language models alone won’t solve the world’s hardest problems – robotics, disease, scientific discovery. Real progress requires models that experience the world and learn from their mistakes, the same way that humans do. And this kind of trial and error can be massively accelerated when done in simulation, rather than in the real world.  World models offer the most clear path to general-purpose simulation, changing how stories are told, how scientific progress is made and how the next frontiers of humanity are reached.    Our team consists of creative, open minded, caring and ambitious people who are determined to change the world. We aspire to continuously build impossible things and our ability to do so relies on building an incredible team. If you are driven to do the same, we'd love to hear from you.    About the role  * Open to hiring remote across the US — we also have offices in NYC, San Francisco, and Seattle   We are hiring a B2B Marketing Dire

title: GCD - Performance, CRM and Scale
description: Please note that we will never request payment or bank account information at any stage of the recruitment process. As we continue to grow our teams, we urge you to be cautious of fraudulent job postings or recruitment activities that misuse our company name and information. title: Creative Director
description: Creative Director Set the vision for how we look, sound, and feel across books, campaigns, and brand. Brave Books publishes children's books and adult nonfiction books. We create subscription services. We run paid ad campaigns. We manage YouTuber relationships where the book is often their first brand extension. Right now, creative direction is distributed — different people making decisions in different directions. We need someone who owns the aesthetic and makes sure every piece of the company reflects a coherent vision. What you'll actually do: World Building. You're the keeper of narrative worlds across our publishing portfolio. For established worlds like Freedom Island, you maintain character bibles, world rules, visual language, and narrative consistency. For new worlds, you help conceptualize them from scratch — understanding the mythology, tone, character archetypes, and rules that make a world feel cohesive and real. You work with writers, illustrators, and product teams to ensure every book extends or reinforces the world it inhabits. Whether it's a YouTuber's first book that launches a new world or the 40th book in an existing universe, you make sure readers feel like they're stepping into something intentional and interconnected, not a random collection of stories. Storytelling Lead. You understand story structure, character development, and narrative arc. For both children's and adult nonfiction books, you'll work with our writers and AI tools to ensure every book has a compelling narrative throughline. For children's books, you'll make sure the story serves the values we're trying to teach. For adult nonfiction, you'll make sure the narrative hooks readers and creates resonance, not just information dumping. You'll give story feedback, shape manuscripts before they're "done," and know when something is working and when it's falling flat. Campaign visual direction. Every launch has a landing page, email sequences, ad creative, and social assets. You'll set the visual direction for each campaign — color palette, photography style, typography — and make sure the execution matches the vision. Brand evolution. Our brand is currently "values-driven children's publisher + adult nonfiction partner." As we scale, are we leaning into that? Are we creating sub-brands? You'll help us think through who we are and how we look. Creator collaboration. When we partner with a YouTuber, they have a visual world — their content style, their audience aesthetic. You'll help our teams understand that world and build book designs that feel authentically theirs, not "made by the publisher." Hire and direct creative freelancers. You'll work with illustrators, designers, photographers, and production vendors. You'll brief them, review their work, give feedback that makes it better, and know when to push and when to step back. Build creative guidelines. Our designers shouldn't have to ask you about every decision. You'll create a shared language — typography standards, color palettes, layout principles — that lets the team make good decisions independently. What we're looking for: A point of view. You have taste. You can articulate why one design works and another doesn't — not "I like this" but "This version creates confidence, clarity, and trust. That one creates confusion." You can defend decisions with reasoning. Appreciation for craft but impatience with vanity. You respect good design, but you know it serves a business function. A beautiful book that doesn't sell is still a failure. Collaboration skills that actually work. You can take feedback without becoming defensive. You can give feedback without crushing people. You can argue for a design direction and then let go when the business case says otherwise. Fluency across mediums. You understand what works on a bookshelf, on an email screen, in an Instagram story, and on an Amazon listing. Different contexts require different approaches. Bonus: Publishing design experience. Children
score_reason: Senior creative leadership with strong integrated scope (brand, narrative, multi-channel), but no explicit remote confirmation, no salary stated, and publishing/books focus is slightly left of Travis's advertising/campaign DNA.

title: Head of Marketing
description: HEAD OF MARKETING, STEVEN.COM COMPANY: STEVEN.COM REPORTING TO: FOUNDER LOCATION: LOS ANGELES OR LONDON BASED MISSION Make Steven.com the definitive brand in the creator economy by building the world's most effective B2B / B2C content engine in our industry. OUTCOMES 1) Brand clarity, coherence, and perception Build and launch a clear, compelling Steven.com brand system - positioning, narrative, tone, and visual identity. You are the architect first. Once the blueprint is set, you'll hire the specialist builders to execute. What success looks like: Everyone internally and externally can describe what Steven.com is, who it's for, and why it exists - and they all say the same thing. Steven.com feels creator-native, trusted, and culturally fluent. Steven.com is recognised as a standalone brand, not an extension of a single individual. It's considered to be the most future-facing, innovative, and pioneering brand in the creator economy. When people in the creator economy are surveyed, Steven.com is the first name they think of - the most known and most respected. Steven.com is the company that creators, brands, and industry partners most want to work with. 2) A story extraction system that feeds the content machine There is no shortage of stories to tell. Across Steven.com at any given time, there are new show releases, new hires, acquisitions, new tools and software launches across platforms like FlightCast.com , new investments, new creative products, and much more. The challenge is building the agentic system that can extract all of these stories from across the organisation, turn them into great content, and get them out across our channels consistently and at pace. What success looks like: A reliable, repeatable process for capturing what's happening across the organisation in real time. No major story, launch, or milestone falls through the cracks. Every part of the business understands how to feed stories into the content pipeline. Raw material is consistently flowing to the content team, ready to be shaped and distributed. 3) A content machine that dominates digital Take the stories coming out of the organisation and turn them into a repeatable, scalable content operation across all major digital channels. We're taking a social-first, digital-first approach - social media, newsletters, video, short-form video, LinkedIn, and the platforms where attention lives today. Over the coming year, we will focus ruthlessly on building the machine to dominate these digital channels. Once that machine is reliable and operating at high quality and cadence, we will expand to other marketing channels. What success looks like: Steven.com has at least 10x the social reach and engagement of any other creator economy company. There is a clear reporting system in place that tracks what content is working, what's driving growth, and how it connects to broader business goals. You have built a marketing team that is one of the best in the company - a team that raises the bar on culture, standards, and output across Steven.com . COMPETENCIES Built a content machine before: You've built or scaled a content operation that predictably produces great work across social, video, and digital channels. You know what it takes to go from zero to a system that runs reliably at pace. Social-native operator: You live and breathe social platforms. You understand how content works natively on each channel, what gets shared, what builds audiences, and you've built systems that scale across them. Brand architect: You've built a brand from the ground up or reshaped one significantly. title: Global Head of Marketing
description: Our mission We're making Africa the first cashless continent. In 2017, over half the population in Sub-Saharan Africa had no bank account. That's for good reason—the fees are too high, the closest branch can be miles away, and nobody takes cards. Without access to financial institutions, people are forced to keep their savings under the mattress. Small business owners rely on lenders who charge extortionate rates. Parents spend hours waiting in line to pay school fees in cash. We're solving this by building financial services that just work: no account fees, instantly available, and accepted everywhere. In places where electricity, water and roads don't always work, you can still send money with Wave. In 2017, we launched a mobile app in Senegal for cash deposit, withdrawal, and peer-to-peer and business payments. Now, we have millions of users across 9 countries and are growing fast. Our goal is to make Africa the first cashless continent and that's where you come in... How You'll Help Us Achieve It We are hiring a Global Head of Marketing to drive growth across Wave’s Payments, Growth, and other core teams. You will report directly to Wave’s General Manager of Growth. Monitor metrics to measure marketing campaign effectiveness and optimize overall marketing performance over time. Continuously build and improve messaging across all channels and target audiences across various segments. Identify and close deals with marketing-specific partners. Optimize the cost of customer acquisition and own marketing budgeting: Collaborate across marketing to establish and manage marketing budgets for each country. Monitor spending to ensure resources are utilized effectively. Identify cost-saving opportunities and allocate budgets strategically to maximize return on investment. Optimize customer acquisition costs by working with our growth team to evaluate campaign performance and channel effectiveness. Manage Wave’s brand: Oversee brand efforts, ensuring consistent and impactful brand communications across all marketing materials and touchpoints. Key details This is a fully remote role. Candidates must be based in one of our talent hub countries (UK, Spain, USA, Canada, Kenya, and Ghana) or in one of our operating markets in Africa, including Senegal, Côte d'Ivoire, or Burkina Faso. Wave provides a yearly $1,200 stipend to support coworking meetups with teammates. Remote team members are expected to travel to our operational markets (e.g., Senegal or Côte d'Ivoire) at least once per quarter. Exceptions apply, but we’ve found this key to understanding our users and product. Our salaries are competitive and are calculated using a transparent formula. For this role, depending on your level and location, we offer a salary up to $195,000, plus a generous equity package Major benefits: Subsidised health insurance for you and your dependents, and retirement contributions (both vary from country to country). 6 months of fully paid parental leave and subsidised fertility assistance. Collaborer avec les parties prenantes des autres équipes artistiques pour s'assurer que les besoins et exigences sont satisfaits afin de livrer les éléments artistiques du jeu. Développer et maintenir les références et la documentation appropriées pour guider l'équipe. Soutenir les chef·fe·s d'équipe artistique et la production pour planifier et mandater le travail de l'équipe. Participer aux révisions pour valider la qualité de la mise en œuvre des départements sous votre responsabilité. Former et responsabiliser les chef·fe·s d’équipe et les contributeur·rice·s individuel·le·s sous votre direction pour intégrer la direction artistique dans leur travail afin de libérer leur créativité et leur sens des responsabilités. Aider à la préparation de la documentation et des présentations. Agir en tant que partie prenante pour le recrutement, l'embauche et l'intégration des nouveaux membres. Contribuer activement au développement des talents, notamment en identifiant les individus à haut potentiel et en soutenant la planification de la relève ainsi que le développement continu des compétences au sein de l'équipe. Ce que nous réaliserons ensemble : Chez Cloud Chamber, nous prônons les hauts standards de qualité en misant sur l’innovation et en cultivant l’agilité face au changement. Nous travaillerons en étroite collaboration et de manière constructive avec les équipes de conception, d'art, d'ingénierie, d'animation et d'audio pour concrétiser nos ambitions créatives. Ensemble, nous resterons à l'affût des avancées technologiques et identifierons des solutions innovantes pour surmonter les obstacles au développement afin de livrer une expérience AAA de calibre mondial. You are fluent in using AI for marketing work — from structuring content for AI search (AEO/GEO) to using AI tools for research, drafting, and workflow automation - You are data-literate: you define KPIs, build dashboards, and use insights to drive your roadmap EXPERIENCE WE'D LOVE TO SEE - 5+ years of B2B marketing experience, ideally in SaaS, proptech, insurtech, or risk/verification products - Direct experience marketing to short-term rental hosts, property managers, or adjacent hospitality/real estate verticals (PMS, channel managers, OTAs, insurance, or fintech for STRs) — strongly preferred - title: Co-Founder & Chief Technology Engineer
description: The opportunity Most independent used-car dealers run their business on a pile of tools that don’t talk to each other. A DMS, a few spreadsheets, a separate CRM, a separate desking tool, a texting app, and a folder full of PDFs. Aerolot replaces all of that with one system: inventory, the sales and F&I funnel, deal desking, lender submission, compliance, and messaging, plus an AI copilot that coaches the dealer while they work a deal. The goal I keep coming back to is that a dealer should never have to open a spreadsheet again. The product already works. It’s about 85% to 90% done and going to market in the next month or two with pilot design partners. What it needs now is a technical co-founder to take it over, get it to launch, and build it out, including the mobile apps that get Aerolot off the desktop and onto the lot. About Aerolot Aerolot is a multi-tenant SaaS platform built specifically for independent used-car dealers, starting in Miami. I built it solo. I know this business from the inside: the sales and F&I funnel, how a DMS actually gets used day to day, lender relationships, F&I product economics, and the compliance rules that touch every deal. That knowledge is the hard part, and a lot of it is already baked into the product. Today the platform has a working inventory and cost system, a deal desk with correct tax and lender math, credit pulls and lender submission, real-time messaging, and Aero, an AI copilot that reads a deal and explains it in plain language. The web app is live in v1. Mobile hasn’t started yet. Why now A few reasons this is worth your time: • The product is built and close to shipping. You inherit a large, working codebase instead of a blank repo, and you call the shots on where it goes from here. • Pilot dealers start in Q3 2026, so your work lands in front of real users fast and the feedback loop is short. • The domain is the hard part, and it’s already worked out. Desking, F&I, lender math, and compliance are mapped, so you get to build good software on top of foundations that are already correct. • This is a founder seat with founder equity. You run engineering and own the technical direction of the company from day one. What you’ll own You are the whole engineering team and the company’s technical voice. That means: • All the code. Frontend, backend, and mobile (iOS and Android). The full stack is yours. • Technical direction. Architecture, stack, tooling, infrastructure, security, and the engineering roadmap. You set the standards and the pace. • Getting it shipped. Taking the product from where it is now to pilot launch, then making it reliable and scalable. • Mobile from scratch. The iOS and Android apps don’t exist yet. You design and build them. • The AI layer. Keeping Aero and the rest of the AI features accurate and dependable. • Bringing me the real options. For any big decision I want the choices, the trade-offs, the costs, and your recommendation, so we make smart calls. What you’ll build Web platform Take the web app the rest of the way. Harden it, scale it, and extend it across inventory, the sales and F&I funnel, deal desk, lender workflows, compliance, and messaging. Tenant isolation is not optional, ever. Backend and infrastructure Own the APIs, the data model, background jobs, integrations, and the infrastructure that keeps a dealer’s day running. Several live third-party integrations (credit and lending, telephony and SMS, payments, market data) have to stay reliable and compliant. Mobile (iOS and Android) Build the mobile experience from nothing. Dealers spend their day on the lot, not behind a desk, so mobile is how they’ll actually use Aerolot. You pick the approach, native or cross-platform, and own it through to the app stores. AI features (Aero) AI isn’t the first thing you’ll touch and it isn’t most of the job, but it’s a feature we have to get right, because it’s where Aerolot does things the old tools can’t. You’ll keep Aero accurate, fast, and trustworthy. It on

title: Brand & Marketing Services Lead
description: Hello. Is it YOU we’re looking for? Cozy greetings from the Mysa team! We’re a company founded on people-first culture, fueled by our mission to lead the global shift toward electrification. Through the creation of life-simplifying smart HVAC controls for homes and businesses, we’re helping our customers save energy, money, and the planet we share. Mysa is looking for a Brand & Marketing Services Lead to join our team in St. John's, NL or Remotely. You’ll own how our brand shows up everywhere a customer meets it. We operate across e-commerce, retail, and utility marketplaces, and this role exists to build one consistent, powerful brand across all of them. You will lead our brand, social, content, product marketing, and creative functions as a shared service that powers every channel we sell through. This is a build role. Our growth to date has come from capturing high-intent, research-driven buyers. The next wave of growth requires reaching customers earlier: educating them, building trust at the top of the funnel, and making the brand work harder across every touchpoint, whether that’s our own store, a retail shelf, or a utility program. One important distinction: you support our channels, you don’t run them. Channel leads own their P&L and execution. You give them a stronger brand, better content, sharper product marketing, and a creative engine that makes every channel more effective. AI is also woven into how we work at Mysa. It isn’t a tool you’ll experiment with - it’s how you’ll scale brand impact, accelerate content creation, strengthen product marketing, and enable every channel we support. Role Overview: Brand: Strategy, positioning, voice, and visual identity. Build a true omnichannel brand with consistency at every touchpoint across e-commerce, retail, and utility marketplaces. Social: Build our organic social presence into a real top-of-funnel asset, tightly integrated with the brand. Content: Own the content strategy that educates the next generation of buyers, moving beyond the self-directed researcher to the mainstream customer. Product marketing: Positioning, messaging, and go-to-market support for new product launches across B2C and B2B. Creative team: Lead the in-house design function as a shared service to all channels and business lines. Key mandates in your first year Establish brand foundations. Set the strategy and guardrails so creative direction debates get settled by the brand, not by opinion. Power top-of-funnel growth. Build the education and awareness engine needed to lower acquisition costs as we scale into new markets and programs. Stand up planning and prioritization. Bring structure to how creative and content work gets scoped, prioritized, and delivered across competing channel demands. Build our in-house video capability. Video is a core gap. You will direct a video creator who works across all three modes: shot, AI-generated, and rendered. More importantly, you will own the campaign direction and prioritization that turns that talent into shipped assets. Your Star Qualities Fully bilingual in French and English (spoken and written). Quebec is central to our growth plans and this role owns how the brand shows up there 5-10 years of progressive experience in brand marketing, product marketing, and creative leadership, with a proven track record of building consumer brands and leading high-performing teams. Proven brand leadership: you’ve built or meaningfully evolved a consumer brand, ideally in hardware, home, or energy. Strong track record of integrating AI into marketing and creative operations, with a clear point of view on how emerging technologies are reshaping brand building and customer acquisition. Experience leading creative teams, including video Product marketing chops across B2C and B2B Strong planning and operational skills: you can run a shared services function serving multiple internal customers without becoming a bottleneck A collaborator’s instinct: success in this role mea

title: Directeur.trice artistique
description: CocoAtelier fait partie du groupe Mastermind Toys, avec 47 magasins à travers le Canada. Nous sommes devenus un écosystème de marques créatives en pleine expansion dans l’univers du lifestyle familial et enfants. Notre portefeuille inclut des marques ambitieuses, digitales et omnicanales : Coco Village – produits design et fonctionnels pour enfants Coco Studio – univers créatif et arts & crafts CAMPUS13 – lifestyle influent pour préados et ados LaJoué – nouveau concept de jouets pour le marché québécois Aperçu du rôle Comme Directeur(trice) Artistique pour Coco Atelier, tu seras le cerveau derrière l’image et l’identité de plusieurs marques : packaging, branding, illustrations, motifs, univers créatifs, visuels produit… bref, tout ce qui fait qu’une marque se démarque et reste en tête. Tu guideras la direction artistique, assureras la cohérence visuelle de nos univers de marque et accompagneras une équipe de designers graphiques dans la création d’emballages, chartes graphiques, motifs, illustrations et contenus visuels distinctifs et inspirants. Tu seras aussi la personne qui donne le ton créatif pour nos marques privées, challenge les idées et aide l’équipe à transformer de bons concepts en univers de marque forts et cohérents. Ce rôle est pour gérer nos projets spéciaux. Ton mandat : faire évoluer l’ADN visuel de notre portefeuille de marques et bâtir des identités fortes, mémorables et alignées sur nos ambitions de croissance. En collaboration étroite avec les équipes produit et créatives, tu contribueras à créer des univers qui attirent l’attention, créent de l’émotion et donnent envie d’y plonger. Responsabilités principales Direction artistique & identité de marque Définir et faire évoluer la direction artistique de plusieurs marques afin de créer des univers visuels forts, cohérents et distinctifs Concevoir des concepts créatifs pour les packagings, guides, étiquettes, supports produits et identités visuelles Développer les moodboards, palettes de couleurs, typographies et lignes directrices visuelles Assurer la cohérence, l’évolution et la pertinence créative de l’ensemble des univers de marque Gestion d’équipe & projets créatifs Encadrer, mobiliser et faire grandir une équipe de designers graphiques Superviser la qualité du travail créatif et offrir une direction claire à l’équipe Gérer les priorités, le flux de travail et les échéanciers dans un environnement multi-projets Collaborer étroitement avec le Gestionnaire de Projet – Packaging & Identité Visuelle, les équipes produit et les partenaires outre-mer Traduire les visions créatives en projets réalisables en tenant compte des réalités de production et des contraintes techniques Qualité visuelle, innovation & exécution Superviser la création de maquettes, motifs, illustrations, layouts d’emballages et visuels graphiques Effectuer la validation créative finale des packagings, visuels et identités graphiques Veiller au respect des standards esthétiques, de l’ADN de marque et des lignes directrices visuelles Participer à la révision des échantillons imprimés afin d’assurer la fidélité des designs et informations Rester à l’affût des tendances design et nourrir l’équipe de références inspirantes pour stimuler l’innovation créative Exigences 5 à 7 ans d’expérience en direction artistique, design graphique ou branding. Portfolio démontrant une forte sensibilité visuelle, du storytelling graphique et un style créatif affirmé. Excellente maîtrise d’Adobe Creative Suite (Illustrator, Photoshop, InDesign). Compréhension des contraintes d’impression, de matériaux et de packaging. Capacité à diriger la création tout en collaborant étroitement avec les designers et chefs de projet. Grande autonomie, créativité débordante, sens du détail et vision stratégique. Très bonnes compétences en communication (français & anglais obligatoires). Ce que nous offrons Horaire de travail flexible Régime d’assurance collective étendu et abordable, incluant la télémédecine et le programme d’ai

You will push the boundaries of what’s possible in marketing creative by harnessing generative AI tools, including Adobe Gen Studio, Claude Cowork, GPT Frontier, Midjourney, VEO, and emerging platforms, to produce scalable, high‑quality visual and multimedia assets. You’ll partner with Brand/Design and Performance Marketing to deliver AI‑generated creative at scale, run rapid experimentation, and build repeatable workflows that democratize creative production across the org. We are looking for a design‑minded technologist who is an avid learner, experimenter, and builder ready to change how creative gets made. This role is equal parts creative vision and technical execution, you will regularly take ideas from zero to working prototype, proving out what's possible before it scales. Responsibilities Generate scalable creative assets including images, video, audio, and interactive content using generative AI platforms (Claude Cowork, Cursor, GPT Frontier, Adobe Gen Studio, Adobe Firefly, Runway, Midjourney, VEO, Flow, Kling, Sora, Profound (GEO), and emerging tools). Take creative concepts from 0 to 1 by rapidly building functional prototypes and proof‑of‑concepts using vibe coding tools (e.g., Claude Code, Cursor, Codex) and Python. Partner with Brand/Design and Performance Media teams to deliver AI‑generated creative for paid campaigns, landing pages, social, and partner marketing. Drive AI‑generated creative from pilot into live, in‑market campaigns and measure impact against real business outcomes: incremental subscribers, conversion rate lift, CAC efficiency, and LTV growth. Own the path from prototype to performance, partnering with Performance Media and Analytics to validate that AI creative is not increasing campaign velocity, but measurably better at driving personalization at scale (revenue/retention). Design and execute rapid A/B/C testing programs for AI‑generated creative, measuring lift in CTR, CVR, and engagement across channels. Build reusable prompt‑based design workflows and creative templates that enable non‑designers across the marketing org to produce on‑brand assets. Develop AI‑generated moodboards, style scoping tools, and interactive content prototypes that accelerate creative development from weeks to days. Collaborate with paid media teams to test AI‑generated creatives across Meta, Google, TikTok, and programmatic platforms; integrate with platform APIs for real‑time performance filtering. Stay current with the rapidly evolving generative creative landscape; evaluate new tools, techniques, and models and recommend adoption strategies. Ensure all AI‑generated creative adheres to Intuit brand guidelines, compliance requirements, and ethical AI standards. Qualifications 4‑5 years of experience in creative production, design technology, or digital marketing, with at least 2 years of hands‑on experience using generative AI tools for creative output. Experience building AI‑based prototypes using Claude Code, Cursor, Loveable or similar tools and going from prototype to MVP leveraging AI tech in marketing and sales functions. Demonstrated proficiency with AI image, video, and audio generation tools (e.g., Midjourney, Runway, VEO, Nano Banana, GPT Image 2.0, Flow, Kling, Sora, Higgsfield Seedance, Stable Diffusion). Proficiency in Python and experience using AI‑assisted coding tools (e.g., Claude code, Cursor, Loveable, v0/Vercel) to quickly prototype ideas and build working creative applications from scratch. Strong visual design sensibility with experience in UX, brand identity, or interactive content development. Experience running creative experimentation programs (A/B/C testing at scale) and measuring performance across digital channels. Proficiency with
score_reason: Strong AI creative fit with deep tool overlap, but individual contributor level (not senior leadership) and missing salary/location clarity prevent higher score.

title: Design Technologist, Amazon Advertising - Brand Innovation Lab
description: Description Advertising Design Technology is Amazon’s custom development and prototyping team for advertisers and agencies. We focus on the conception, prototyping and execution of branded custom experiences across Amazon properties. As a Design Technologist you will split your time between execution and strategy. You will be contributing to the definition of new ad products, pre-sales prototypes and custom campaign execution, all while working directly with Designers, Product Managers, Engineers and Account teams. You will have comprehensive understanding of the current state (and future) of web technology with a critical awareness of implications on user-centered design. You feel comfortable reading, writing, and reviewing vanilla Typescript/JavaScript both on the client and server, building your own libraries and creating task automation (specifically with AI) to speed up development. You are customer-obsessed, detail-oriented, comfortable working on highly technical teams and are skilled in using data and insights to make decisions. You feel comfortable in writing documentation and handling multiple projects in a fast-paced environment with a high-level of autonomy. You are equally passionate about design and development. You have strong front-end proficiency, are a creative problem solver at the core and have a keen eye for world class functional design. You will bring disruptive ideas for how to conceive, create, and deliver branded shopping experiences for millions of customers worldwide and work across desktop, mobile, Fire tablet, Fire TV, Alexa, Out of Home and emerging devices. Basic Qualifications 3+ years of front-end technologist, engineer, or UX prototyper experience Have an available online portfolio Have coding samples in front end programming languages Experience with web, mobile web, or mobile app front-end development Experience with UI design for complex workflows and for end users Preferred Qualifications Knowledge of technologies such as JavaScript/NodeJS, HTML/CSS, Responsive/Adaptive Design Knowledge of Python, Javascript, and source control (Git) Amazon is an equal opportunity employer and does not discriminate on the basis of protected veteran status, disability, or other legally protected status. Our inclusive culture empowers Amazonians to deliver the best results for our customers. If you have a disability and need a workplace accommodation or adjustment during the application and hiring process, including support for the interview or onboarding process, please visit https://amazon.jobs/content/en/how-we-hire/accommodations for more information. If the country/region you’re applying in isn’t listed, please contact your Recruiting Partner. The base salary range for this position is listed below. Your Amazon package will include sign-on payments and restricted stock units (RSUs). Final compensation will be determined based on factors including experience, qualifications, and location. Amazon also offers comprehensive benefits including health insurance (medical, dental, vision, prescription, Basic Life & AD&D insurance and option for Supplemental life plans, EAP, Mental Health Support, Medical Advice Line, Flexible Spending Accounts, Adoption and Surrogacy Reimbursement coverage), 401(k) matching, paid time off, and parental leave. Learn more about our benefits at https://amazon.jobs/en/benefits. USA, NY, New York - 145,000.00 - 196,100.00 USD annually USA, WA, SEATTLE - 131,800.00 - 178,300.00 USD annually Company - Amazon Advertising LLC Job ID: A3196316

At Beast Industries, we believe in the transformative power of digital media and its potential to entertain, educate, and effect positive change. Our commitment to innovation, creativity, and philanthropy drives us to explore new frontiers, create unforgettable experiences, and build a legacy that inspires future generations. Shape the Public Reputation of One of the Most Influential Brands in the World We're looking for a strategic communications leader to own and evolve how Beast Industries shows up in real-time public conversations. As Director, Corporate Communications & Social Reputation, you will lead the strategy, systems, and governance that shape our corporate reputation across social platforms, executive channels, and emerging public forums. Reporting directly to the Chief Communications Officer, you'll serve as a trusted advisor to the CEO, Founder, and executive leadership team, helping navigate an increasingly complex communications landscape while building long-term trust, credibility, and brand affinity. This role sits at the intersection of corporate communications, executive visibility, social reputation, and issues management, turning moments of attention into opportunities to strengthen the Beast Industries brand. This is a highly visible leadership role for a communications professional who combines exceptional judgment, platform fluency, executive presence, and strategic thinking. You will help define how one of the world's most recognized creator-led companies engages with audiences, manages reputation, and participates in culture at scale. Positioning, messaging, brand voice in partnership with our Creative Alchemist, social, content, email, paid where it makes sense, and the website. Designing and operating the modern AI-leveraged GTM stack. Clay, Apollo, Smartlead, Instantly, n8n, Claude, ElevenLabs, and whatever else proves out. You will build workflows we haven't thought of yet. Translating product reality into market reality. Working closely with our product, engineering, and creative teams so what we ship is what we can sell, and what we sell is what we ship. Building the commercial operating system. Partnership economics, pipeline forecasts, and the commercial reporting cadence that lets the leadership team make good decisions. Hiring your team when the math says it's time , and not before. Who you are: 10+ years across some combination of marketing, sales, BD, growth, or RevOps , with proven depth in at least two and demonstrated capability across three. Founding-team or early-stage experience. A track record of being employee #5 to #50 at a fast-growing tech company, ideally B2B SaaS or platform. You know what it feels like to be the entire commercial team. Demonstrably AI-native. You don’t just use AI tools. You have opinions about them,
